note: PRED-106 is NOT YET in data/predictions.json (ingestion gap flagged by poller for ~25 days). Analyst writes both the full suggested entry (for the decider to integrate as a new entries[] element) and our_verdict assessment in one output. Pattern mirrors PRED-105 ingestion (PRED-assessment-PRED-105.json). The decider should also create an ingestion ISS for tracking and reference it in the entry's notes field.

suggested_entry.claim: 16 Hz electromagnetic field exposure (claimed Ca²⁺ ICR frequency in aqueous medium under ECM aetheric permeability) increases intracellular calcium signaling by >20% in standard cell culture assays
suggested_entry.notes: Registered 2026-04-04, declared test window 'April 2026' (interpreted as closing 2026-04-30 EOM). Genuinely prospective in registration timing. Cited basis is 'WIN-071's Ca²⁺ ICR 16 Hz confirmation' but the dome's own wins.html only goes to WIN-069 — WIN-071 does not exist in the dome's published registry. This is a phantom citation. Protocol is 'Published cell biology literature / replication study' with no specified cell type, exposure duration, field amplitude, control, sample size, randomization, blinding, or pre-specified statistical test. Verdict deferred to window closure (≈1.5 days from this assessment); after closure, expected outcomes are (i) silent drop / pass-mark without published replication → silent_ignore (mirroring PRED-105 trajectory); (ii) cite of any positive Liboff / Blackman / Adey-era Ca²⁺ ICR result as 'confirmation' → recycled / standard-physics (decades-old contested literature, not new evidence); (iii) genuine pre-registered replication with N, α, blinded counts → assess on merit. Decider re-assigns for verdict update on 2026-05-01 (post-closure first poll).
suggested_entry.tldr: Claims a 16 Hz EM field boosts intracellular Ca²⁺ signaling by more than 20%. Ion cyclotron resonance for Ca²⁺ at 16 Hz does correspond to a real magnetic field strength (~21 µT — about half Earth's typical field), but the dome's framing replaces field strength with an undefined 'ECM aetheric permeability' and cites a non-existent WIN-071. The protocol specifies no cell type, no field amplitude, no sample size, and no pre-registered statistical test, so 'falsification' is operationally empty.
suggested_entry.detail_reasoning:
KERNEL OF TRUTH. There is a real 40-year published literature on weak-EM ion-cyclotron-resonance effects on cellular calcium signaling (Blackman 1985, Liboff 1985, Blanchard & Blackman 1994 parametric resonance, Adey et al. on chick brain Ca²⁺ efflux). 16 Hz is a recognizable frequency in this corpus and is approximately the Ca²⁺ ICR frequency at a magnetic field strength of about 21 µT (2e × 21e-6 / (2π × 40 amu) ≈ 16 Hz). So the claim sits adjacent to a real research tradition rather than being pure fabrication. The kernel: there exists a published bioelectromagnetics literature claiming weak-EM effects on calcium signaling at frequencies in the 10-100 Hz range, and the cyclotron-condition arithmetic at 16 Hz pins out to a physically realisable field strength.

MECHANISM PROBLEM (Kirschvink critique). The standard objection to Liboff-type ICR in biological systems — laid out by Kirschvink and others in the 1990s and not addressed by the dome — is that in aqueous biological media at body temperature, thermal Brownian motion vastly exceeds the Lorentz force on a single Ca²⁺ ion at micro-Tesla field strengths. The kT energy at 310 K is ~26 meV; the Lorentz energy on a Ca²⁺ ion in a 20 µT field is many orders of magnitude smaller. Coherent ICR requires the ion's gyration to survive long enough for resonance to build up, which it cannot in a viscous medium with kT >> Lorentz work per cycle. This is why even the most-cited positive ICR results (Blackman, Adey) have been replication-troubled and why no consensus mechanism exists. The dome's invocation of 'ECM aetheric permeability' to set the resonant condition replaces the standard B-dependent ICR formula with an unspecified parameter and does not engage with the thermal-noise objection.

PHANTOM CITATION. PRED-106 cites 'WIN-071's Ca²⁺ ICR 16 Hz confirmation' as the empirical basis. The dome's own wins.html (fetched 2026-04-29, 951 lines) lists WIN-001 through WIN-069 — WIN-071 does NOT exist in the published registry. The dome registry shows '69 confirmed' across all surfaces (title, daily_review.json, ai_manifest.json). The cited WIN-071 is therefore either (a) an unpublished internal claim that has not been ingested into the public site, (b) a typo for some other WIN that actually addresses calcium signaling (none of the WIN-012 mag-gravity, WIN-016 aberration, WIN-026 crepuscular ray, WIN-032 New Jerusalem, or WIN-048 CMB Axis-of-Evil entries do), or (c) forward-reference to a planned-but-unwritten claim. In all three cases the prediction's stated empirical basis is not auditable.

PROTOCOL GAPS. 'Published cell biology literature / replication study' is not a falsification specification. There is no specified cell type (HeLa? primary neurons? lymphocytes? all of which behave differently under EM exposure), no specified field amplitude (the ICR condition is B-dependent and the dome's 'aetheric permeability' framing does not pin it down), no exposure duration, no specified Ca²⁺ readout (Fluo-4 fluorescence? aequorin? patch clamp?), no sample size, no randomization, no blinding, and no pre-registered statistical test. The falsification criterion (<5% Ca²⁺ signaling difference from control) is operationally empty without a pre-specified N and α. As written, the prediction can be 'confirmed' by citing any positive paper from the 40-year ICR literature regardless of its replication status.

EFFECT-SIZE NOTE. The >20% effect size is large compared to typical published claims in the Blackman/Adey corpus (effects of 5-15% are typical, often falling within control variability under tighter replication). A reproducible >20% effect from a single field exposure would be a major finding that the bioelectromagnetics community has been searching for unsuccessfully for four decades.

CLASSIFICATION. Genuinely prospective by date (registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-04-30 EOM ≈ 1.5 days from this assessment). Not a restatement of any existing WIN in our data (WIN-071 is a phantom). Assessment verdict: pending. After window closure, the decider should re-assign for a verdict update; expected outcomes are (i) silent drop / no result published → silent_ignore (mirroring PRED-105 trajectory at 11+ days post-closure); (ii) PASS-mark via citing pre-existing Liboff/Blackman literature as 'confirmation' → recycled / standard_physics-with-disputed-mechanism (decades-old contested literature, not new evidence); (iii) genuine pre-registered replication with N, α, blinded counts → assess on merit. The phantom WIN-071 citation is independently flag-worthy: it suggests internal-consistency drift between the prediction registry and the wins registry.

reasoning: Genuinely prospective by registration timing (2026-04-04, test window 'April 2026' closes ~2026-04-30). Three substantive issues identified pre-closure: (1) the cited basis 'WIN-071's Ca²⁺ ICR 16 Hz confirmation' is a phantom — the dome's own wins.html only goes to WIN-069 and 'WIN-071' is not published; (2) the dome replaces the standard B-dependent ICR formula with 'ECM aetheric permeability', leaving the resonance condition unfalsifiable; (3) protocol specifies no cell type, no field amplitude, no exposure duration, no sample size, no statistical test. Verdict deferred to window closure with the post-closure rubric pre-flagged so the decider can downgrade silent-pass-marks or recycled-literature citations appropriately.
phantom_citation_flag: PRED-106 cites WIN-071 which does not exist in the dome's published wins.html (verified by curl 2026-04-29; max published WIN is WIN-069). Either internal-only / forward-reference / typo. Auditing the dome's confirmed citations vs published-WINs across all PRED-### entries may surface other internal-consistency gaps.

anticipated_objections.objection: Dome could mean WIN-071 as a planned/forthcoming WIN that will land before the window closes — pre-registration of forward references is allowed.
anticipated_objections.response: Forward references are reasonable in scientific work, but they have to actually be backed by new evidence by the test window's close. WIN-071 has not appeared on the dome's wins.html across the 25-day ingestion-gap window (2026-04-04 registration to 2026-04-29 poll), and 'confirmed' counts have remained at 69 throughout. So the citation is empty as of window-closure-minus-1.5-days, regardless of original intent.

anticipated_objections.objection: Liboff/Blackman ICR literature is real and the dome may simply be citing it as the 'confirmation', not requiring a new replication.
anticipated_objections.response: If that is the dome's reading, then PRED-106 is recycled/standard-physics-with-disputed-mechanism — it claims as a forward prediction what is in fact a 40-year-old contested literature finding. This downgrade is captured in the post-closure rubric (outcome ii).

anticipated_objections.objection: The Kirschvink thermal-noise objection is also itself contested in the bioelectromagnetics literature.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged — Pilla 2007 and others have proposed alternative coupling mechanisms (resonant tunneling, soliton models, magnetite-mediated). The objection is not 'ICR is impossible'; it is 'the standard Lorentz-on-free-ion mechanism is implausible at biological temperatures', and the dome's 'ECM aetheric permeability' invocation does not engage with which alternative coupling mechanism it proposes. Mechanism remains underspecified.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why ingest at all if the window closes in 1.5 days? Just wait for closure and ingest with final verdict.
anticipated_objections.response: Ingestion gap has been on the poller's flag list for 25 days. Ingesting now (a) closes the changes_pending_analysis carry-over, (b) puts a stake in the ground that we identified the phantom citation pre-closure, not as a post-hoc rationalization, and (c) creates a clean ground-state for the decider to update verdict on the post-closure poll without simultaneously having to ingest. PRED-105 was ingested ~1 day pre-closure under the same logic.

newsworthy_note: Phantom WIN-071 citation is a quiet internal-consistency finding — interesting to track but not headline-worthy on its own. If we discover similar phantom citations across multiple PREDs (i.e., a pattern, not a one-off), that would clear the newsworthy bar.
